preview_text:
The model backs Croatia heavily, assigning a 79% win probability against a 3% chance for Ghana. This reflects a substantial gap between the two sides, leaving Ghana with minimal winning odds under the statistical framework.

The win-draw-loss spread shows Croatia as clear favourite, yet the draw carries meaningful weight at 19%. A competitive first half remains plausible, though the model expects Croatia to pull clear if the match develops. The outcome distribution suggests Ghana's path to points runs primarily through a stalemate rather than victory.

The model and market diverge significantly here. Sportsbooks price Croatia at 58% and Ghana at 17%, implying much tighter margins than the model's 79–3% split. Polymarket consensus lies between the two, at 60% Croatia and 15% Ghana. The dashboard does not claim to know which source better captures match reality; the gap warrants attention from users assessing their own conviction.
